description: A numerical cognition lab has collected dot enumeration data using the PEBL Subitizing test to study the parallel visual processing limit. Using the dataset at ~/pebl/data/enumeration_data.csv (containing participant_id, trial, dot_count, response, correct, and rt_ms), generate a JSON report at ~/pebl/analysis/subitizing_report.json. The analysis requires excluding participants who engaged in response mashing (near 100% accuracy for 1 dot, but 0% accuracy for >1 dot, with suspiciously fast RTs). For all valid participants, calculate 'subitizing_slope_ms' (linear regression of RT vs dot_count for 1-3 dots) and 'counting_slope_ms' (linear regression for 5-8 dots) using only correct trials. The report must also include accuracy proportions for the small range (1-4 dots) as 'accuracy_1_to_4' and the large range (5-9 dots) as 'accuracy_5_to_9', along with group means for both slopes across all valid participants. The output JSON should include a list of participants with their respective slopes, accuracies, or exclusion status and reason, followed by the group mean values.

metadata.data_source: Domain-specific generative psychometric model based on Mandler & Shebo (1982)
